ctxMarket: Attention is on outflows from major crypto ETFs, with Solana, Bitcoin, and Ethereum funds seeing significant redemptions. Solana's price has dropped to its lowest levels since 2023, while Hyperliquid's HYPE token has overtaken SOL in price, though not in market cap.
ctxTalking:
• Major ETF outflows impacting Solana, Bitcoin, and Ethereum products
• Solana's price decline and HYPE's rise in the market
• New Solana-native features: onchain perps trading and recurring payments
ctxMatters: ETF outflows may affect liquidity and sentiment for Solana-related products. The introduction of native subscriptions, allowances, and perps trading expands Solana’s onchain utility. Builders are focusing on new primitives and financial products, including tokenized assets like gold.
ctxSignal:
Signal: ETF redemptions and new onchain features are shaping Solana’s ecosystem and user experience.
Noise: Short-term price speculation around SOL’s next move.
ctxGlossary:
ETF — Exchange-traded fund, a tradable investment product tracking crypto or other assets
Onchain perps — Perpetual futures contracts settled directly on a blockchain

readMore.title: BTC, ETH, SOL and XRP ETFs bleed $4.4 billion over 13 sessions, only HYPE in green
Hyperliquid’s HYPE overtakes Solana in price as SOL falls to lowest levels since 2023
